output: T14 completed. I found existing ESLint flat-config infrastructure in `reigh-app/eslint.config.js`, so I added the Dialog guard there instead of a grep fallback. The new `no-restricted-syntax` rule flags `if (!open)` / `if (!isOpen)` null returns only when they sit immediately before a `return <Dialog ...>`. I added one violating fixture, one compliant fixture, and a small verification script. Validation passed: the dedicated fixture-check script confirmed the invalid fixture fails and the valid fixture passes; direct ESLint on the fixtures reported exactly one error on the violating fixture; and targeted ESLint on the real dialog files stayed clean.

task_updates.executor_notes: Detected existing ESLint flat-config infrastructure in `eslint.config.js`, so I added the guard there instead of a grep fallback. The new `no-restricted-syntax` selector flags `if (!open)` / `if (!isOpen)` null returns only when they immediately precede a `return <Dialog ...>`. Added one violating fixture and one compliant fixture under `eslint-rules/__fixtures__/`, then verified the behavior two ways: the dedicated `node scripts/quality/check-dialog-early-return-rule.mjs` script passed, and direct ESLint against the fixtures reported exactly one error on the violating fixture while the compliant fixture stayed clean. Targeted ESLint on the real dialog files also passed.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed. The dedicated fixture-check script passes, and direct ESLint on the fixtures reports the new `no-restricted-syntax` error on `dialog-early-return.invalid.tsx` while `dialog-early-return.valid.tsx` remains clean.
